message:
feat(components): wire spinner and progress bars to centralized animation detection

Spinner, ProgressBar, and IndeterminateBar now consult
styles.AnimationsEnabled() to decide whether to schedule tick commands
and advance animation frames:

- Spinner.Init(), Update(SpinnerTickMsg), and tick() all short-circuit
  with nil commands when animations are disabled, preventing CPU-wasting
  goroutine chains that repaint static content.
- ProgressBar defaults its Animated field from AnimationsEnabled() &&
  SupportsTrueColor() instead of unconditional true. Update handler
  also guards against stale tick messages when Animated is false.
- IndeterminateBar gains an Animated field (defaulted from
  AnimationsEnabled()) with matching guards in Init() and Update().

Tests:
- New enableAnimationsForTest() helper sets the full env matrix
  (NTM_ANIMATIONS=1, TMUX="", STY="", CI="", TERM=xterm-256color,
  COLORTERM=truecolor) so existing animation-dependent tests pass
  under the stricter detection.
- New TestSpinnerDisablesAnimationInTmuxByDefault verifies Init()
  returns nil and Update does not advance frames inside tmux.
- New TestProgressBarDisablesAnimationInTmuxByDefault and
  TestIndeterminateBarDisablesAnimationInTmuxByDefault verify the
  same no-tick behavior for both progress bar types.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
